steps.title: Finding $a$ by matching the $x^3$ coefficient
steps.content:
When we expand $(ax + 2)(7x^2 - bx + 5)$, the $x^3$ term comes from $ax \cdot 7x^2 = 7ax^3$.

The right side has $21x^3$.

Therefore: $$7a = 21 \quad \Rightarrow \quad a = 3$$

steps.title: Finding $ab$ by matching the $x^2$ coefficient
steps.content:
When we expand, the $x^2$ terms come from:
  • $ax\cdot (-bx) = -abx^2$
  • $2\cdot 7x^2 = 14x^2$
Combined, the $x^2$ coefficient is $-ab+14$. The right side has coefficient $-16$, so:
$$-ab+14=-16$$
$$ab=30$$
This matches, so C is correct.
